_metadata.description: Eutelsat OneWeb constellation orbital shell parameters compiled from Wikipedia and public filings.
notes: Gen 1 constellation completed in late 2024 with 648 satellites (600 active + 48 in-orbit spares).
OneWeb merged with Eutelsat in September 2023, forming 'Eutelsat Group'.
Gen 1 satellites have NO inter-satellite links; traffic is routed through gateway ground stations.
Gen 2 constellation of up to 6,372 satellites filed with FCC in January 2021.
Satellites built by OneWeb Satellites, a joint venture of Airbus and OneWeb.

shells.notes: Originally planned 18 planes x 49 sats (882 total); reduced to 12 planes during restructuring. Near-polar orbit provides near-global coverage.
FCC filing January 2021 for up to 6,372 Gen2 satellites. Orbital parameters not fully public. Gen2 satellites expected to include ISL capability.

derived_orbital_parameters.description: Pre-computed orbital parameters for Gen1 shell

gen1.notes: No ISL; all traffic routed through gateway ground stations. Ku-band user beams, Ka-band gateway feeder links.

gen2.notes: JoeySat test satellite (~500 kg) launched May 2023.
